content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here, you need to output a valid sequence of operations too.**

You are given 222 binary strings AAA and BBB, both of length NNN.

You can perform the following operation on AAA multiple times (possibly 000):


Choose LLL and RRR such that 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N, and flip all the values AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,ARA_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_RAL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã.
The cost of this operation is 111 if AL=ARA_L = A_RAL‚Äã=AR‚Äã, and 000 otherwise.
Flipping a value means changing it to a 111 if it was 000, and vice versa.

What is the minimum total cost of operations to transform AAA to BBB. Construct a valid sequence of operations of length at most 500050005000 achieving that cost.

It can be proven that it is always possible to transform AAA to BBB, and at least one optimal sequence of operations exists with length less than 500050005000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string AAA.
The third line contains a binary string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



On the first line, output the minimum cost of transforming AAA to BBB.
On the second line, output KKK (0‚â§K‚â§50000 \le K \le 50000‚â§K‚â§5000), the number of operations you want to perform.
Output KKK lines, each containing 222 integers LLL and RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N), representing the substring you have chosen for this operation.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: First, we do the operation on L=2L = 2L=2 and R=3R = 3R=3 to get A=001A = 001A=001, and then on L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3 to get A=110A = 110A=110. This makes AAA equal to BBB for 000 cost.
